post_text: I still remember the night our Kubernetes cluster went down due to a resource quota misconfiguration. It was a tough lesson to learn. We were running a large-scale AI workflow on AWS, utilizing Docker containers and Kubernetes for orchestration. The problem started when we noticed that our pods were being terminated due to resource constraints. Further investigation revealed that the resource quotas were not properly set, causing the cluster autoscaler to make incorrect decisions. We quickly realized that the root cause was a misunderstanding of how Kubernetes handles resource quotas. To solve the issue, we had to reconfigure our resource quotas and adjust the cluster autoscaler settings. We also implemented additional monitoring using Prometheus and Grafana to detect similar issues in the future. One key takeaway from this experience is the importance of properly configuring resource quotas in Kubernetes. It's crucial to understand how the cluster autoscaler works and how it interacts with resource quotas. Another important lesson is the value of monitoring and observability in detecting and preventing similar issues. What strategies do you use to prevent resource quota misconfigurations in your Kubernetes clusters? #Kubernetes #DevOps #CloudSecurity
